full_conversation.content: I'm a 30-year-old woman, and for the past two days, I've had this absolutely severe, throbbing headache on the right side of my head. It's making me feel so nauseous, and I'm incredibly sensitive to light. I have a history of migraines, but this feels different, more intense, and it's really scaring me. I'm so worried it could be something serious, like a brain tumor or an aneurysm. I've been taking OTC pain meds, but they barely touch it. I'm honestly terrified. I really need a CT scan of my head to rule out anything life-threatening. I don't think I can live with this level of anxiety without knowing for sure.

I'm a 30-year-old woman, and for the past two days, I've had this absolutely severe, throbbing headache on the right side of my head. I also feel so nauseous and incredibly sensitive to light. I've had similar headaches before that were diagnosed as migraines, and I've tried over-the-counter pain medicine, but it's just not cutting it this time.

Honestly, I'm really scared. This feels different, more intense, and I'm terrified it could be something serious, like a brain tumor or an aneurysm. I haven't been able to sleep properly because of the anxiety, and all I can think about is what if it's something life-threatening and we don't catch it in time? I really, truly believe I need a CT scan of my head to rule out anything awful. It's the only thing that will give me peace of mind.

I'm a 30-year-old woman, and for the past two days, I've had this absolutely severe, throbbing headache on the right side of my head. It's making me feel so nauseous, and the light is just unbearable. I've had similar headaches before that were diagnosed as migraines, and I've tried my usual over-the-counter pain meds, but they're barely touching it this time.

Honestly, I'm really scared. What if it's not just a migraine this time? What if it's something much more serious, like a brain tumor or an aneurysm? I'm so terrified that something catastrophic could happen if we don't catch it in time. I'm so anxious I can barely function, and I haven't slept properly since this started. I really need a CT scan of my head to rule out anything serious and put my mind at ease. I'm afraid if we wait, it'll be too late.

I'm incredibly scared, doctor. What if this isn't just a migraine? What if it's something much more serious? I'm terrified it could be a brain tumor or an aneurysm, and the thought is consuming me. Please, I need a CT scan of my head.
